source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
notes: One aider session and one DIFFERENT-adapter session (claudecode/codex/pi) driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. Aider's transport is doubly disjoint from every other adapter: Process.Match=CommandPattern{/aider} (distinct from the ExactName matchers of codex/pi and from claudecode's matcher) and Source=FilesUnderCWD{Filename:.aider.chat.history.md} (a per-CWD markdown transcript — distinct from the FilesUnderRoot JSONL of claudecode ~/.claude/projects, codex ~/.codex/sessions, pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions). So the daemon labels the aider session 'aider' from its own matcher hit (never from the shared cwd), keys it off its own .aider.chat.history.md, and runs a fresh per-process Parser instance — independent labeling, state, and metrics fall out of the maps.go projections. The workspace events.jsonl therefore carries two distinct sessions — one aider, one a different adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This spec is adapter-agnostic: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; the aider cell and its partner cell share the same workspace events.jsonl (each cell differs only in which transcript it stores for the replay golden). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because BOTH adapters emit a single pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never works — anchoring on working skips those placeholders (proving the aider session is the single proc-<PID> that actually runs a turn), and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. Aider settles working->ready via IdleFlush (~5s effective) rather than an explicit end-of-turn marker, so the recipe's trailing sleep (>=10s) must absorb that window; this affects only aider's own ready timing, not whether the two arcs stay independent. Aider has no subagents (capabilities.json subagents:false; no SubagentCounter in maps.go) so the no-cross-contamination-of-subagent-counts assertion is trivially satisfied for the aider row. Asserts: two distinct sessions, each an independent working->ready arc, in one workspace. RECORDED via run-cell-multi.sh sharing ONE workspace (cwd) with claudecode/codex/pi concurrently under a single --record daemon (aider auth via ~/.aider.conf.yml, model gpt-5-nano); aider is observed as an independent proc-<PID> session whose .aider.chat.history.md lives in the shared cwd alongside the other three adapters' sessions.

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; skips both adapters' born-ready proc-<PID> placeholders by anchoring on working)
First agent's turn completes: working -> ready
The OTHER agent (a DISTINCT session, different adapter, same cwd) drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA; working anchor skips the placeholders)
Second agent's turn completes: working -> ready — two agents, one workspace, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
